title: Kubernetes Node Pressure Conditions Memory Disk PID and NetworkUnavailable Proactive Eviction Risk
exclusions: Does not replace UC-3.2.3 Node NotReady terminal readiness failures, UC-3.2.46 Cluster Autoscaler or Karpenter scale-out refusal narratives, UC-3.2.16 bound PVC filesystem exhaustion forecasts, UC-3.2.33 voluntary cordon and drain workflows, or UC-3.2.44 managed node pool auto-repair replacements. Does not by itself prove underlying hardware faults or silent packet loss without sibling network telemetry. Does not perform eviction or scaling actions automatically; it produces evidence for human-approved remediation.
evidence: Saved search uc_3_2_28_node_pressure_proactive with five-minute schedule; versioned lookups/node_inventory.csv; weekly CSV export of the closing table to a restricted evidence index with kube-state-metrics chart version recorded in the description field.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a non-production worker, induce MemoryPressure or DiskPressure per kubelet documentation while kube-state-metrics and node_exporter scrapes remain healthy; confirm k8s_metrics shows kube_node_status_condition with the expected condition true, confirm node_exporter percentages move in the opposing direction, confirm kube:events may emit EvictionThresholdMet, and confirm uc_3_2_28_node_pressure_proactive returns a row with non-zero pressure_minutes, populated qos_at_risk_pods, and severity at least medium.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Cordon a node without resource exhaustion; confirm kubelet conditions do not report sustained MemoryPressure DiskPressure PIDPressure or NetworkUnavailable solely from cordon semantics and the saved search stays quiet unless independent pressure signals appear.

dataSources: index=k8s_metrics sourcetype=prometheus:scrape:metrics from Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector scrapes of kube-state-metrics kube_node_status_condition for MemoryPressure DiskPressure PIDPressure and NetworkUnavailable when status true, kube_pod_info for qos_class priority and node placement, and node_exporter node_memory_MemAvailable_bytes node_memory_MemTotal_bytes node_filesystem_avail_bytes node_filesystem_size_bytes node_processes_pids and max process gauges; index=k8s_events or index=k8s sourcetype=kube:events for Node involvedObject with reasons EvictionThresholdMet NodeHasSufficientMemory ImageGCFailed EvictionAuthFailed; governance join via lookups/node_inventory.csv for node_pool node_priority drain_policy and pool cardinality.
app: Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector for Kubernetes (Splunk Add-on for Kubernetes documentation) with prometheus_simple or prometheus receiver scraping kube-state-metrics and node_exporter, k8s_events receiver for kube:events, splunk_hec exporter into k8s_metrics and k8s_events; kube-state-metrics deployment with node and pod collectors per upstream RBAC.
spl:
`comment("UC-3.2.28 Kubernetes node pressure Memory Disk PID NetworkUnavailable. tstats anchor on k8s_metrics; multisearch merges kube_node_status_condition series, node_exporter memory filesystem pid series, kube_pod_info QoS, kube:events eviction edges. Tunables earliest=-30m@m pool_frac_warn=0.30 dwell_warn_min=5")`
| tstats latest(_time) AS ksm_wallclock WHERE index=k8s_metrics sourcetype="prometheus:scrape:metrics" earliest=-30m@m latest=now BY cluster
| join type=inner max=0 cluster [| multisearch
    [ search index=k8s_metrics sourcetype="prometheus:scrape:metrics" earliest=-30m@m latest=now
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__,metric_name,name,""))))
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name,cluster_name,cluster,eks_cluster_name,gke_cluster_name,aks_cluster_name,resource_cluster,""))))
      | eval nodex=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(node,node_name,kubernetes_io_hostname,k8s_node_name,instance,""))))
      | rex field=_raw "condition=\"(?<cond_lab>[^\"]+)\""
      | rex field=_raw "status=\"(?<stat_lab>[^\"]+)\""
      | eval ks_cond=trim(toString(coalesce(condition,cond_lab,"")))
      | eval ks_stat=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(status,stat_lab,""))))
      | rex field=_raw "(?<metval>[0-9]+(?:\.[0-9]+)?)\s*$"
      | eval metric_val=tonumber(metval,10)
      | where like(mn,"kube_node_status_condition") AND ks_cond IN ("MemoryPressure","DiskPressure","PIDPressure","NetworkUnavailable") AND ks_stat IN ("true","1") AND isnotnull(metric_val) AND metric_val>=1 AND len(cluster)>0 AND len(nodex)>0
      | eval memory_pressure=if(ks_cond=="MemoryPressure",1,0)
      | eval disk_pressure=if(ks_cond=="DiskPressure",1,0)
      | eval pid_pressure=if(ks_cond=="PIDPressure",1,0)
      | eval network_unavailable=if(ks_cond=="NetworkUnavailable",1,0)
      | rename nodex AS node
      | bucket _time span=1m
      | stats max(memory_pressure) AS memory_pressure max(disk_pressure) AS disk_pressure max(pid_pressure) AS pid_pressure max(network_unavailable) AS network_unavailable BY cluster node _time ]
    [ search index=k8s_metrics sourcetype="prometheus:scrape:metrics" earliest=-30m@m latest=now
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__,metric_name,name,""))))
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name,cluster_name,cluster,eks_cluster_name,gke_cluster_name,aks_cluster_name,resource_cluster,""))))
      | eval nodex=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(node,node_name,kubernetes_io_hostname,k8s_node_name,instance,""))))
      | rex field=_raw "(?<metval>[0-9]+(?:\.[0-9]+)?)\s*$"
      | eval v=tonumber(coalesce(value,metric_value,metval,""),10)
      | where len(nodex)>0 AND len(cluster)>0 AND (match(mn,"node_memory_memavailable_bytes|node_memory_mem_available_bytes|node_memory_memtotal_bytes|node_memory_mem_total_bytes|node_filesystem_avail_bytes|node_filesystem_size_bytes|node_processes_pids|node_processes_max_processes|process_max_processes"))
      | rename nodex AS node
      | bucket _time span=1m
      | eval mem_avail_b=if(match(mn,"memavailable|mem_available"),v,null())
      | eval mem_total_b=if(match(mn,"memtotal|mem_total") AND !match(mn,"available"),v,null())
      | eval fs_avail_b=if(match(mn,"filesystem_avail"),v,null())
      | eval fs_size_b=if(match(mn,"filesystem_size_bytes"),v,null())
      | eval proc_pids=if(mn="node_processes_pids",v,null())
      | eval proc_max=if(match(mn,"max_processes"),v,null())
      | stats latest(mem_avail_b) AS mem_avail_b latest(mem_total_b) AS mem_total_b latest(fs_avail_b) AS fs_avail_b latest(fs_size_b) AS fs_size_b latest(proc_pids) AS proc_pids latest(proc_max) AS proc_max BY cluster node _time ]
    [ search index=k8s_metrics sourcetype="prometheus:scrape:metrics" earliest=-30m@m latest=now
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__,metric_name,name,""))))
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name,cluster_name,cluster,eks_cluster_name,gke_cluster_name,aks_cluster_name,resource_cluster,""))))
      | eval nodex=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(node,node_name,kubernetes_io_hostname,k8s_node_name,instance,""))))
      | rex field=_raw "pod=\"(?<pod_nm>[^\"]+)\""
      | rex field=_raw "namespace=\"(?<pod_ns>[^\"]+)\""
      | rex field=_raw "qos_class=\"(?<qos_lab>[^\"]+)\""
      | eval qos=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(qos_class,qos_lab,""))))
      | eval pri=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(priority,""))),10)
      | where like(mn,"kube_pod_info") AND len(pod_nm)>0 AND len(nodex)>0
      | rename nodex AS node
      | eval be=if(qos="besteffort",1,0)
      | eval bu=if(qos="burstable",1,0)
      | eval gu=if(qos="guaranteed",1,0)
      | bucket _time span=1m
      | stats dc(pod_nm) AS total_pods sum(be) AS cnt_be sum(bu) AS cnt_bu sum(gu) AS cnt_gu min(pri) AS min_pri BY cluster node _time ]
  | sort 0 cluster node + _time
  | stats max(memory_pressure) AS memory_pressure max(disk_pressure) AS disk_pressure max(pid_pressure) AS pid_pressure max(network_unavailable) AS network_unavailable max(mem_avail_b) AS mem_avail_b max(mem_total_b) AS mem_total_b max(fs_avail_b) AS fs_avail_b max(fs_size_b) AS fs_size_b max(proc_pids) AS proc_pids max(proc_max) AS proc_max max(total_pods) AS total_pods max(cnt_be) AS cnt_be max(cnt_bu) AS cnt_bu max(cnt_gu) AS cnt_gu min(min_pri) AS min_pri BY cluster node _time
  | eval mem_avail_pct=if(isnotnull(mem_total_b) AND mem_total_b>0 AND isnotnull(mem_avail_b), round(100*mem_avail_b/mem_total_b,2), null())
  | eval fs_avail_pct=if(isnotnull(fs_size_b) AND fs_size_b>0 AND isnotnull(fs_avail_b), round(100*fs_avail_b/fs_size_b,2), null())
  | eval pid_avail_pct=if(isnotnull(proc_max) AND proc_max>0 AND isnotnull(proc_pids), round(100*(proc_max-proc_pids)/proc_max,2), null())
  | eval qos_at_risk_pods=printf("BE:%s BU:%s GU:%s pri_min:%s", tostring(coalesce(cnt_be,0)), tostring(coalesce(cnt_bu,0)), tostring(coalesce(cnt_gu,0)), tostring(coalesce(min_pri,"na")))
  | eval evict_order_first="BestEffort_then_Burstable_then_Guaranteed_lowest_scheduling_priority_first"
  | eval any_pressure=if(memory_pressure>0 OR disk_pressure>0 OR pid_pressure>0 OR network_unavailable>0,1,0)
  | sort 0 cluster node + _time
  | streamstats current=f global=f last(eval(if(any_pressure<1,_time,null()))) AS last_clear_t BY cluster node
  | eval dwell_anchor=coalesce(last_clear_t,relative_time(now(),"-30m@m"))
  | eval pressure_minutes=if(any_pressure>0, round((_time-dwell_anchor)/60,2), 0)
  | sort 0 cluster node - _time
  | dedup cluster node ]
| join type=left max=0 cluster node [| search (index=k8s_events OR index=k8s) sourcetype="kube:events" earliest=-30m@m latest=now
  | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name,cluster_name,cluster,source_cluster,""))))
  | eval nodex=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(involvedObject.name,object_ref_name,host,""))))
  | eval ik=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(involvedObject.kind,involvedObject_kind,""))))
  | eval reason_ev=trim(toString(coalesce(reason,type,"")))
  | where ik="node" AND reason_ev IN ("EvictionThresholdMet","NodeHasSufficientMemory","ImageGCFailed","EvictionAuthFailed")
  | rename nodex AS node
  | stats values(reason_ev) AS ev_reasons latest(_time) AS ev_last BY cluster node ]
| join type=left max=0 cluster node [| inputlookup node_inventory.csv
  | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(cluster)))
  | eval node=lower(trim(toString(node)))
  | eval node_pool=toString(coalesce(node_pool,pool,nodegroup,"unknown_pool"))
  | eval node_priority=toString(coalesce(node_priority,priority_class,scheduling_priority,"unknown_pri"))
  | eval drain_policy=toString(coalesce(drain_policy,cordon_policy,"standard_drain"))
  | fields cluster node node_pool node_priority drain_policy ]
| join type=left max=0 cluster node_pool [| inputlookup node_inventory.csv
  | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(cluster)))
  | eval node_pool=toString(coalesce(node_pool,pool,nodegroup,"unknown_pool"))
  | stats dc(node) AS pool_total_nodes BY cluster node_pool ]
| eventstats dc(node) AS pool_pressured_nodes BY cluster node_pool
| eval pool_pressure_frac=if(isnotnull(pool_total_nodes) AND pool_total_nodes>0, round(pool_pressured_nodes/pool_total_nodes,4), null())
| eval severity=case(
    match(lower(mvjoin(ev_reasons,"")),"imagegcfailed"), "critical",
    pool_pressure_frac>=0.30 AND pressure_minutes>=5, "critical",
    memory_pressure>0 AND pressure_minutes>=5 AND match(lower(node_priority),"(?i)tier|gold|prod|0"), "critical",
    disk_pressure>0 AND isnotnull(fs_avail_pct) AND fs_avail_pct<=5, "high",
    pid_pressure>0 AND isnotnull(pid_avail_pct) AND pid_avail_pct<=10, "high",
    network_unavailable>0 AND pressure_minutes>=3, "high",
    memory_pressure>0 AND pressure_minutes>=5, "high",
    any_pressure>0 AND pressure_minutes>=5, "medium",
    any_pressure>0, "low",
    true(), "informational")
| where severity IN ("critical","high","medium") AND any_pressure>0
| table cluster node node_pool memory_pressure disk_pressure pid_pressure network_unavailable pressure_minutes mem_avail_pct fs_avail_pct pid_avail_pct qos_at_risk_pods total_pods evict_order_first severity
description: Watches kubelet-reported node conditions before Ready goes false: MemoryPressure, DiskPressure, PIDPressure, and NetworkUnavailable from kube-state-metrics kube_node_status_condition samples that read true while the node may still accept scheduling, then tracks how long those flags stay set inside the analysis window. The analytic fuses one-minute multisearch arms for condition gauges, node_exporter memory filesystem and pid headroom percentages, and kube_pod_info counts by qos_class so responders see which BestEffort and Burstable pods face earliest eviction alongside minimum observed priority. streamstats approximates continuous dwell since the last clear minute, eventstats and inventory-backed pool totals estimate what fraction of each node pool is simultaneously pressured to justify pre-emptive scale-out aligned with UC-3.2.46 when scheduling backlog follows, and kube event reasons add kubelet edges such as EvictionThresholdMet, ImageGCFailed, EvictionAuthFailed, and NodeHasSufficientMemory recovery.
value: Gives platform and service teams a countdown story before involuntary eviction and NotReady: pressure dwell, pool saturation fraction, host headroom from node_exporter that should corroborate kubelet claims, and a QoS-shaped inventory of pods still colocated on the node so leaders can drain deliberately, add nodes, or shed BestEffort work without rediscovering the situation only after pods disappear. Reliability improves when memory disk pid and network attachment stress is treated as early warning rather than as a surprise once UC-3.2.3 fires. Capacity owners gain numeric pool_pressure_frac evidence to prioritize cluster-autoscaler or cloud scale-out investigations using UC-3.2.46 when pending appears. Audit and post-mortem reviewers can show proactive monitoring of kubelet conditions correlated with telemetry that explains whether pressure was real or a sensor mismatch.
implementation: Stand up k8s_metrics and k8s_events with HEC tokens; scrape kube-state-metrics and node_exporter via Splunk OTel Collector; ship Node kube events; publish node_inventory.csv with pools and priorities; save uc_3_2_28_node_pressure_proactive every five minutes on earliest=-30m@m; route critical pressure and thirty-percent pool fractions to platform and open UC-3.2.46 when scale-out fails.
visualization: Heatmap of pool_pressure_frac by node_pool; timeline of pressure_minutes by node; single value of pressured nodes; table matching the closing SPL columns with drilldowns to kube:events and k8s_metrics raw lines for the same node and minute.
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Application_State.state) AS app_state latest(Application_State.info) AS app_info FROM datamodel=Application_State WHERE nodename=Application_State (Application_State.app="kubernetes" OR Application_State.app="k8s" OR like(Application_State.app,"%kube%")) earliest=-4h@h latest=@h BY Application_State.dest
| rename Application_State.dest AS cim_node
| join type=left max=0 cim_node
    [| tstats summariesonly=t avg(Performance.mem_used_percent) AS mem_used_pct avg(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_load_pct max(Performance.disk_usage) AS disk_usage_pct FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-4h@h latest=@h BY Performance.host
     | rename Performance.host AS cim_node ]
| where like(lower(app_state),"%memory%pressure%") OR like(lower(app_info),"%disk%pressure%") OR like(lower(app_info),"%pid%pressure%") OR like(lower(app_info),"%network%unavailable%") OR mem_used_pct>92 OR disk_usage_pct>92
| table cim_node app_state app_info mem_used_pct cpu_load_pct disk_usage_pct
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Splunk CIM Application_State and Performance summaries on entities mapped to Kubernetes node host records so tstats overlays stay warm when correlating kubelet pressure narratives with host saturation; retain at least thirty days for quarterly reliability reviews.

references.title: Splunk Documentation — Splunk Add-on for Kubernetes

knownFalsePositives: Cron-driven log rotation and brief image pull bursts can flip DiskPressure true for under a minute while kubelet reclaims logs or layers; require sustained dwell and maintenance correlation before paging production bridges, and document PassDoNotAlert patterns for approved compression jobs. Dedicated build and continuous integration pools often run intentionally hot memory and scratch disks; tie those node_inventory rows to dev or build criticality so alerts route to platform dashboards instead of customer bridges unless SLO namespaces colocate there. DaemonSet startup can spike process counts transiently; pair PIDPressure with minute-level dwell and with process leader identity from workload metadata before blaming applications. kube-image-puller or bootstrap bursts on fresh nodes can distort disk and pid telemetry for a few minutes after join; dampen alerts for a short post-bootstrap window keyed from cloud launch timestamps when available. Scratch-disk-only ephemeral storage configurations on development nodes routinely flirt with DiskPressure without customer impact; separate pools in inventory and suppress except when gold namespaces appear on those nodes by mistake. NetworkUnavailable often appears during cluster-managed restarts of CNI agents or during rolling upgrades of the data plane; align to provider maintenance feeds and suppress when Ready remains true and east-west probes stay healthy. Spot interruption notices may correlate with pressure flaps; downgrade when termination is expected. Duplicate prometheus scrapes without dedup keys can double-count pods in qos_at_risk_pods; validate one authoritative kube-state-metrics scrape. Test namespaces that intentionally run unlimited BestEffort noise will page; exclude via lookup flags. After kube-state-metrics upgrades, metric label renames may null coalesce paths briefly; treat missing qos_at_risk_pods as telemetry debt not silence. When swap is enabled against best practice, MemoryPressure semantics diverge from linux free memory intuition; annotate fleet policy in inventory notes.

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: optional — bind node pool entities when episode policies should open on sustained pool_pressure_frac burn

detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this proactive node-pressure control together with Kubernetes platform SRE, observability engineers operating Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector fleets, and capacity owners who approve cluster-autoscaler scale-out when pools overheat before Ready flips false. This use case is intentionally not UC-3.2.3 Node NotReady, which is the failure-already-happened axis when the kubelet stops posting Ready true. It is not UC-3.2.46, which is cluster-level autoscaler refusal after scheduling pressure manifests as Pending backlog. It is not UC-3.2.16, which is PVC byte and inode exhaustion on bound volumes rather than whole-node root or imagefs pressure. It is not UC-3.2.33 voluntary drain, and not UC-3.2.44 managed auto-repair replacement semantics. The unique signal lane is kube-state-metrics kube_node_status_condition rows where condition is MemoryPressure, DiskPressure, PIDPressure, or NetworkUnavailable and status reads true, meaning the control plane has accepted kubelet claims that eviction, garbage collection, fork limits, or CNI attachment health are in a dangerous band while the node may still report Ready true.

Index contracts mirror sibling cat-3 gold patterns. index=k8s_metrics ingests prometheus:scrape:metrics lines from kube-state-metrics and node_exporter DaemonSets scraped by Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector prometheus_simple or prometheus receiver jobs. index=k8s_events or index=k8s with sourcetype kube:events carries Node involvedObject kinds with reasons such as EvictionThresholdMet, NodeHasSufficientMemory after recovery, ImageGCFailed when reclaim breaks, and EvictionAuthFailed when RBAC or policy blocks eviction. Retention should keep at least fourteen days of metrics for dwell math and thirty days of events for post-mortems. RBAC on collectors must allow list watch on nodes and pods for kube-state-metrics, and host-level scrape paths for node_exporter without granting cluster-admin on forwarding credentials.

kube-state-metrics must expose kube_node_status_condition with labels for condition and status, kube_pod_info with qos_class and priority when your chart version includes those labels, and optionally kube_node_info for validation joins. node_exporter should expose node_memory_MemAvailable_bytes or node_memory_MemAvailable_bytes style names depending on exporter version, node_filesystem_avail_bytes paired with node_filesystem_size_bytes for the root or kubelet-relevant mount, node_processes_pids with a coherent maximum from node_processes_max_processes or platform-specific pid_max series so pid_avail_pct is not nonsense. Governance lookup lookups/node_inventory.csv must include cluster, node, node_pool, node_priority or tier semantics, drain_policy or cordon_policy hints, owner_team, criticality, and allow_reboot_window style fields used by sibling UCs even when this SPL only consumes node_pool, node_priority, and drain_policy in the join arm.

Risk briefing: MemoryPressure true means the kubelet is telling the apiserver that reclaimable memory is below eviction thresholds and BestEffort pods are first in line for eviction when pressure persists. DiskPressure true means image garbage collection and log reclaim are imminent; ephemeral writes and image layers can fail. PIDPressure true means the process id space is tight; churny workloads may fail to fork children. NetworkUnavailable true often precedes Ready flips when the CNI has not programmed the node data plane yet or lost it during agent restarts. Operators should treat sustained pressure as the window to drain gracefully, add capacity, or shed BestEffort before UC-3.2.3 fires.

Licensing and cardinality: pressure analytics multiply series per node per condition. Use Prometheus relabel keep lists that retain condition and status labels on kube_node_status_condition and avoid exploding custom labels onto node_exporter. Dual scrapes of the same target without deduplication distort streamstats dwell; enforce one authoritative scrape path per cluster.

Training alignment: teach incident staff that kubectl describe node surfaces the same condition booleans customers expect in dashboards, and that Splunk rows should agree within one or two scrape intervals. FinOps alignment: when thirty percent of a pool shows pressure longer than five minutes, this UC escalates with pool_pressure_frac to justify UC-3.2.46 scale-out work rather than blaming individual microservices first.

Differentiation recap: proactive kubelet-reported pressure and dwell before NotReady, cross-checked with node_exporter capacity percentages and pod QoS inventory, with pool-level fraction to trigger capacity conversations tied to UC-3.2.46, not terminal NotReady in UC-3.2.3.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Deploy kube-state-metrics with the node collector enabled and RBAC that permits nodes and pods list watch. ServiceMonitor or PodMonitor should scrape kube-state-metrics every fifteen to thirty seconds on path /metrics with honor_labels discipline consistent across environments. Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector DaemonSets should scrape node_exporter on each worker host and kube-state-metrics via cluster receiver or in-cluster Service DNS. Preserve k8s_cluster_name or equivalent external labels on every scrape so cluster coalesce ladders in SPL stay short.

Collector YAML sketch for prometheus receiver scraping kube-state-metrics and node_exporter targets with splunk_hec exporters routing to k8s_metrics and sourcetype prometheus:scrape:metrics appears in platform runbooks with collection_interval thirty seconds, insecure TLS only where threat models allow, bearer tokens for kubelet-adjacent scrapes, and explicit index routing for k8s_metrics.

k8s_events receiver should run in watch mode so Node events with eviction reasons arrive with the same cluster field you use in metrics. props and transforms must normalize __name__, value, node, and cluster onto indexed fields for fast tstats opening arms.

node_inventory.csv schema sample:

cluster,node,node_pool,node_priority,drain_policy,owner_team,criticality
prod-eks-01,ip-10-0-1-42.ec2.internal,workers-amd64,tier1,standard_drain,platform-core,gold
prod-eks-01,ip-10-0-9-03.ec2.internal,build-amd64,dev,relaxed_drain,build,silver

Validation before alert authoring: run index=k8s_metrics kube_node_status_condition earliest=-15m, index=k8s_metrics node_memory earliest=-15m, index=k8s_events EvictionThresholdMet earliest=-15m. Skew between collectors should remain under sixty seconds or dwell math misleads on-call.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save as uc_3_2_28_node_pressure_proactive with a five-minute schedule, dispatch earliest=-30m@m, dispatch latest=now, and throttle duplicate cluster node rows for ten minutes unless severity escalates from medium to high or pool_pressure_frac crosses thirty percent. Include qos_at_risk_pods, pressure_minutes, and pool_pressure_frac in ticket narratives. Route critical ImageGCFailed or gold-tier sustained MemoryPressure to platform and storage bridges; route pool-level thirty percent pressure to capacity owners with explicit pointer to open UC-3.2.46 scale-out diagnostics when Pending backlog appears.

Pipeline narrative for operators: the opening tstats arm proves recent k8s_metrics ingest for each cluster before the inner multisearch runs, avoiding silent false negatives when indexes are mistyped during drills. The multisearch fuses three metric planes at one-minute granularity: kube_node_status_condition true samples for the four pressure conditions, node_exporter memory filesystem and pid series with coalesce-friendly metric names, and kube_pod_info counts split by qos_class with a minimum observed priority for eviction ordering stories. After the fan-in stats by cluster node _time, mem_avail_pct, fs_avail_pct, and pid_avail_pct quantify host headroom that should move in the opposite direction of kubelet pressure flags when telemetry is healthy. streamstats tracks the last minute any_pressure was zero per node so pressure_minutes approximates continuous dwell in the pressured state inside the search window; when pressure never clears, dwell_anchor falls back to the window start. After dedup to the latest minute per node, a left join on kube:events collects EvictionThresholdMet, NodeHasSufficientMemory, ImageGCFailed, and EvictionAuthFailed reasons for kubelet correlation without claiming the Events API is exhaustive for every eviction. inputlookup node_inventory.csv enriches node_pool, node_priority, and drain_policy for routing macros. A second inventory join supplies pool_total_nodes per cluster and node_pool so pool_pressure_frac compares pressured node cardinality to fleet inventory rather than to whatever happened to appear in raw scrapes. eventstats counts pool_pressured_nodes per pool from the alert candidate set. case assigns severity with ImageGCFailed and widespread pool pressure first, then gold-tier memory dwell, tight filesystem or pid percentages, NetworkUnavailable dwell, generic memory dwell, medium for any sustained pressure, and low filtered out at the where clause together with informational. The closing table lists exactly fifteen analyst columns: cluster, node, node_pool, memory_pressure, disk_pressure, pid_pressure, network_unavailable, pressure_minutes, mem_avail_pct, fs_avail_pct, pid_avail_pct, qos_at_risk_pods, total_pods, evict_order_first, severity.

Paste-and-run SPL for alerts and dashboards must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-3.2.28 Kubernetes node pressure Memory Disk PID NetworkUnavailable. tstats anchor on k8s_metrics; multisearch merges kube_node_status_condition series, node_exporter memory filesystem pid series, kube_pod_info QoS, kube:events eviction edges. Tunables earliest=-30m@m pool_frac_warn=0.30 dwell_warn_min=5")`
| tstats latest(_time) AS ksm_wallclock WHERE index=k8s_metrics sourcetype="prometheus:scrape:metrics" earliest=-30m@m latest=now BY cluster
| join type=inner max=0 cluster [| multisearch
    [ search index=k8s_metrics sourcetype="prometheus:scrape:metrics" earliest=-30m@m latest=now
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__,metric_name,name,""))))
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name,cluster_name,cluster,eks_cluster_name,gke_cluster_name,aks_cluster_name,resource_cluster,""))))
      | eval nodex=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(node,node_name,kubernetes_io_hostname,k8s_node_name,instance,""))))
      | rex field=_raw "condition=\"(?<cond_lab>[^\"]+)\""
      | rex field=_raw "status=\"(?<stat_lab>[^\"]+)\""
      | eval ks_cond=trim(toString(coalesce(condition,cond_lab,"")))
      | eval ks_stat=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(status,stat_lab,""))))
      | rex field=_raw "(?<metval>[0-9]+(?:\.[0-9]+)?)\s*$"
      | eval metric_val=tonumber(metval,10)
      | where like(mn,"kube_node_status_condition") AND ks_cond IN ("MemoryPressure","DiskPressure","PIDPressure","NetworkUnavailable") AND ks_stat IN ("true","1") AND isnotnull(metric_val) AND metric_val>=1 AND len(cluster)>0 AND len(nodex)>0
      | eval memory_pressure=if(ks_cond=="MemoryPressure",1,0)
      | eval disk_pressure=if(ks_cond=="DiskPressure",1,0)
      | eval pid_pressure=if(ks_cond=="PIDPressure",1,0)
      | eval network_unavailable=if(ks_cond=="NetworkUnavailable",1,0)
      | rename nodex AS node
      | bucket _time span=1m
      | stats max(memory_pressure) AS memory_pressure max(disk_pressure) AS disk_pressure max(pid_pressure) AS pid_pressure max(network_unavailable) AS network_unavailable BY cluster node _time ]
    [ search index=k8s_metrics sourcetype="prometheus:scrape:metrics" earliest=-30m@m latest=now
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__,metric_name,name,""))))
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name,cluster_name,cluster,eks_cluster_name,gke_cluster_name,aks_cluster_name,resource_cluster,""))))
      | eval nodex=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(node,node_name,kubernetes_io_hostname,k8s_node_name,instance,""))))
      | rex field=_raw "(?<metval>[0-9]+(?:\.[0-9]+)?)\s*$"
      | eval v=tonumber(coalesce(value,metric_value,metval,""),10)
      | where len(nodex)>0 AND len(cluster)>0 AND (match(mn,"node_memory_memavailable_bytes|node_memory_mem_available_bytes|node_memory_memtotal_bytes|node_memory_mem_total_bytes|node_filesystem_avail_bytes|node_filesystem_size_bytes|node_processes_pids|node_processes_max_processes|process_max_processes"))
      | rename nodex AS node
      | bucket _time span=1m
      | eval mem_avail_b=if(match(mn,"memavailable|mem_available"),v,null())
      | eval mem_total_b=if(match(mn,"memtotal|mem_total") AND !match(mn,"available"),v,null())
      | eval fs_avail_b=if(match(mn,"filesystem_avail"),v,null())
      | eval fs_size_b=if(match(mn,"filesystem_size_bytes"),v,null())
      | eval proc_pids=if(mn="node_processes_pids",v,null())
      | eval proc_max=if(match(mn,"max_processes"),v,null())
      | stats latest(mem_avail_b) AS mem_avail_b latest(mem_total_b) AS mem_total_b latest(fs_avail_b) AS fs_avail_b latest(fs_size_b) AS fs_size_b latest(proc_pids) AS proc_pids latest(proc_max) AS proc_max BY cluster node _time ]
    [ search index=k8s_metrics sourcetype="prometheus:scrape:metrics" earliest=-30m@m latest=now
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__,metric_name,name,""))))
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name,cluster_name,cluster,eks_cluster_name,gke_cluster_name,aks_cluster_name,resource_cluster,""))))
      | eval nodex=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(node,node_name,kubernetes_io_hostname,k8s_node_name,instance,""))))
      | rex field=_raw "pod=\"(?<pod_nm>[^\"]+)\""
      | rex field=_raw "namespace=\"(?<pod_ns>[^\"]+)\""
      | rex field=_raw "qos_class=\"(?<qos_lab>[^\"]+)\""
      | eval qos=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(qos_class,qos_lab,""))))
      | eval pri=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(priority,""))),10)
      | where like(mn,"kube_pod_info") AND len(pod_nm)>0 AND len(nodex)>0
      | rename nodex AS node
      | eval be=if(qos="besteffort",1,0)
      | eval bu=if(qos="burstable",1,0)
      | eval gu=if(qos="guaranteed",1,0)
      | bucket _time span=1m
      | stats dc(pod_nm) AS total_pods sum(be) AS cnt_be sum(bu) AS cnt_bu sum(gu) AS cnt_gu min(pri) AS min_pri BY cluster node _time ]
  | sort 0 cluster node + _time
  | stats max(memory_pressure) AS memory_pressure max(disk_pressure) AS disk_pressure max(pid_pressure) AS pid_pressure max(network_unavailable) AS network_unavailable max(mem_avail_b) AS mem_avail_b max(mem_total_b) AS mem_total_b max(fs_avail_b) AS fs_avail_b max(fs_size_b) AS fs_size_b max(proc_pids) AS proc_pids max(proc_max) AS proc_max max(total_pods) AS total_pods max(cnt_be) AS cnt_be max(cnt_bu) AS cnt_bu max(cnt_gu) AS cnt_gu min(min_pri) AS min_pri BY cluster node _time
  | eval mem_avail_pct=if(isnotnull(mem_total_b) AND mem_total_b>0 AND isnotnull(mem_avail_b), round(100*mem_avail_b/mem_total_b,2), null())
  | eval fs_avail_pct=if(isnotnull(fs_size_b) AND fs_size_b>0 AND isnotnull(fs_avail_b), round(100*fs_avail_b/fs_size_b,2), null())
  | eval pid_avail_pct=if(isnotnull(proc_max) AND proc_max>0 AND isnotnull(proc_pids), round(100*(proc_max-proc_pids)/proc_max,2), null())
  | eval qos_at_risk_pods=printf("BE:%s BU:%s GU:%s pri_min:%s", tostring(coalesce(cnt_be,0)), tostring(coalesce(cnt_bu,0)), tostring(coalesce(cnt_gu,0)), tostring(coalesce(min_pri,"na")))
  | eval evict_order_first="BestEffort_then_Burstable_then_Guaranteed_lowest_scheduling_priority_first"
  | eval any_pressure=if(memory_pressure>0 OR disk_pressure>0 OR pid_pressure>0 OR network_unavailable>0,1,0)
  | sort 0 cluster node + _time
  | streamstats current=f global=f last(eval(if(any_pressure<1,_time,null()))) AS last_clear_t BY cluster node
  | eval dwell_anchor=coalesce(last_clear_t,relative_time(now(),"-30m@m"))
  | eval pressure_minutes=if(any_pressure>0, round((_time-dwell_anchor)/60,2), 0)
  | sort 0 cluster node - _time
  | dedup cluster node ]
| join type=left max=0 cluster node [| search (index=k8s_events OR index=k8s) sourcetype="kube:events" earliest=-30m@m latest=now
  | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(k8s_cluster_name,cluster_name,cluster,source_cluster,""))))
  | eval nodex=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(involvedObject.name,object_ref_name,host,""))))
  | eval ik=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(involvedObject.kind,involvedObject_kind,""))))
  | eval reason_ev=trim(toString(coalesce(reason,type,"")))
  | where ik="node" AND reason_ev IN ("EvictionThresholdMet","NodeHasSufficientMemory","ImageGCFailed","EvictionAuthFailed")
  | rename nodex AS node
  | stats values(reason_ev) AS ev_reasons latest(_time) AS ev_last BY cluster node ]
| join type=left max=0 cluster node [| inputlookup node_inventory.csv
  | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(cluster)))
  | eval node=lower(trim(toString(node)))
  | eval node_pool=toString(coalesce(node_pool,pool,nodegroup,"unknown_pool"))
  | eval node_priority=toString(coalesce(node_priority,priority_class,scheduling_priority,"unknown_pri"))
  | eval drain_policy=toString(coalesce(drain_policy,cordon_policy,"standard_drain"))
  | fields cluster node node_pool node_priority drain_policy ]
| join type=left max=0 cluster node_pool [| inputlookup node_inventory.csv
  | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(cluster)))
  | eval node_pool=toString(coalesce(node_pool,pool,nodegroup,"unknown_pool"))
  | stats dc(node) AS pool_total_nodes BY cluster node_pool ]
| eventstats dc(node) AS pool_pressured_nodes BY cluster node_pool
| eval pool_pressure_frac=if(isnotnull(pool_total_nodes) AND pool_total_nodes>0, round(pool_pressured_nodes/pool_total_nodes,4), null())
| eval severity=case(
    match(lower(mvjoin(ev_reasons,"")),"imagegcfailed"), "critical",
    pool_pressure_frac>=0.30 AND pressure_minutes>=5, "critical",
    memory_pressure>0 AND pressure_minutes>=5 AND match(lower(node_priority),"(?i)tier|gold|prod|0"), "critical",
    disk_pressure>0 AND isnotnull(fs_avail_pct) AND fs_avail_pct<=5, "high",
    pid_pressure>0 AND isnotnull(pid_avail_pct) AND pid_avail_pct<=10, "high",
    network_unavailable>0 AND pressure_minutes>=3, "high",
    memory_pressure>0 AND pressure_minutes>=5, "high",
    any_pressure>0 AND pressure_minutes>=5, "medium",
    any_pressure>0, "low",
    true(), "informational")
| where severity IN ("critical","high","medium") AND any_pressure>0
| table cluster node node_pool memory_pressure disk_pressure pid_pressure network_unavailable pressure_minutes mem_avail_pct fs_avail_pct pid_avail_pct qos_at_risk_pods total_pods evict_order_first severity
```

savedsearches.conf sketch: schedule every five minutes, alert when severity is critical or high, include cluster, node, node_pool, pressure_minutes, pool_pressure_frac, and ev_reasons mvjoin in the notable description.

Operational notes: when mem_avail_pct stays high while MemoryPressure is true, suspect cgroup-level limits, kernel accounting differences, or mis-scraped MemAvailable versus kubelet eviction thresholds; open a sensor drift case from Step 5. When fs_avail_pct disagrees with DiskPressure, validate which mount node_exporter tracks versus imagefs and nodefs split configuration on the kubelet. When pid_avail_pct is null because proc_max is missing, rely on PIDPressure and kube events while fixing exporter config.

Step 4 — Validate

Synthetic memory pressure in non-production: use a lab node labeled disposable, run a controlled memory allocator inside a BestEffort pod without limits until MemoryPressure toggles true in kubectl describe node, confirm kube_node_status_condition appears in k8s_metrics with condition MemoryPressure and status true, confirm mem_avail_pct falls, and confirm uc_3_2_28_node_pressure_proactive returns a row with qos_at_risk_pods reflecting BestEffort counts. Remove the workload and confirm NodeHasSufficientMemory or condition clear within expected scrape intervals and that the saved search clears when severity drops.

Synthetic disk pressure: fill node-local ephemeral or imagefs according to your kubelet eviction configuration in lab only, watch DiskPressure true, correlate fs_avail_pct decline, and verify ImageGCFailed appears if you intentionally break garbage collection permissions in a sandbox only. Restore disk and confirm DiskPressure clears.

Synthetic pid pressure: spawn many short-lived processes in a controlled cgroup in lab, raise PIDPressure, validate pid_avail_pct compression, then tear down.

Synthetic NetworkUnavailable: during approved CNI maintenance or lab chaos, observe NetworkUnavailable true while Ready may still be true briefly, confirm network_unavailable column and severity logic, then recover agents.

Negative control: cordon-only nodes without resource pressure should not produce sustained pressure flags; if alerts fire solely on cordon, revisit inventory drain_policy suppressions and UC-3.2.33 separation.

Correlation: compare Splunk timestamps to kubectl describe node condition transitions and to journalctl kubelet logs mentioning eviction thresholds.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — MemoryPressure on a tier-1 production node with qos_at_risk_pods showing high BestEffort counts: treat as imminent eviction risk, drain BestEffort and low-priority Burstable first, add capacity if pool_pressure_frac is climbing, and page service owners whose pods lack requests.

Case 2 — Transient DiskPressure during cron-driven log rotation that clears within two minutes: classify as PassDoNotAlert in macros tied to known logrotate windows and maintenance calendars; require sustained dwell above five minutes before paging in gold pools.

Case 3 — DiskPressure with ImageGCFailed in ev_reasons: prioritize kubelet and container runtime health, registry reachability, and disk inode exhaustion on imagefs; escalate critical severity even when mem_avail_pct looks comfortable.

Case 4 — Sustained PIDPressure resembling fork-heavy workloads or accidental fork bombs: isolate noisy namespaces, review ulimits and sysctl pid_max, consider cgroup pids.max, and validate daemonsets for process leaks.

Case 5 — NetworkUnavailable during cluster-managed CNI agent restarts: align to provider maintenance bulletins, suppress short flaps under documented NetworkUnavailable maintenance macros, and watch for transition into UC-3.2.3 NotReady if heartbeats stop entirely.

Case 6 — MemoryPressure leading to eviction and workload reschedule onto neighbors that then pressure: follow eviction events to destination nodes, correlate with UC-3.2.46 if Pending backlog appears because autoscaler cannot add nodes fast enough, and expand pools before cascading pressure.

Case 7 — DiskPressure on dedicated build or test pools with scratch-disk-only ephemeral storage: mark node_inventory rows with dev_pool semantics and PassDoNotAlert patterns unless duration exceeds policy; still log evidence for FinOps.

Case 8 — Thirty percent of a node pool pressured longer than five minutes: open a capacity incident, invoke UC-3.2.46 diagnostics for scale-out failures, and approve pre-emptive node group growth or instance type changes.

Case 9 — NodeHasSufficientMemory recovery edge after reclaim: downgrade severity automatically on clear, capture the recovery minute in post-mortems, and verify workloads stopped thrashing.

Case 10 — kubelet stuck reporting NotReady while pressure flags stay false: pivot to UC-3.2.3 for terminal readiness failures; do not assume this UC will explain the outage.

Case 11 — Pressure metrics disagree with node_exporter view across multiple scrapes: treat as sensor drift or threshold misconfiguration, open a telemetry ticket, and temporarily require dual corroboration before muting.

Case 12 — Pool_pressure_frac false positives when inventory is stale: refresh node_inventory.csv from cloud APIs before muting alerts; stale inventory underestimates pool_total_nodes and can hide true thirty percent events.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 includes a fenced spl block identical to the spl JSON field; Step 5 lists twelve Case lines using the Case N — pattern; monitoringType lists Reliability and Availability; cimModels lists Performance and Application_State; equipmentModels lists kubernetes_k8s.
grandmaExplanation: Each computer in the cluster can quietly run out of memory, disk space, process slots, or network wiring before it is marked broken. We watch those early warning lights, count how long they stay on, and list which programs would be moved first if space is not freed.
hardware: Applies to Linux worker nodes on Amazon EKS, Google GKE, Microsoft AKS, Red Hat OpenShift, VMware Tanzu, and kubeadm clusters where kube-state-metrics and node_exporter reach Splunk with consistent labels; Arm and x86 SKUs are in scope when metric names remain consistent.
